name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram Milwaukee, Wisconsin — MoneyGram operates over 13,000 locations across the United States offering money transfer, check cashing, and bill paym...
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer and financial services company with an extensive physical footprint across the United States. The company has built its reputation on providing accessible financial services through a nationwide network of agent locations, making their services convenient for consumers who prefer in-person transactions or lack traditional banking access.

MoneyGram's core offerings include sending and receiving money nationwide, check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment services. The company operates both physical locations and an online platform, allowing customers to choose between digital and in-person service delivery based on their preferences and needs. Their service model focuses on accessibility and convenience rather than competitive pricing or innovative features.

MoneyGram distinguishes itself through its massive location network—over 13,000 locations across the United States—providing unmatched physical accessibility compared to digital-only competitors. This extensive agent network means customers can access services quickly without relying on bank branches or online-only platforms. The company's dual online-and-offline model attempts to serve both digitally-savvy and traditional consumers.

However, MoneyGram's fees for money transfer and check cashing services are typically higher than bank alternatives or online competitors. The company's primary value proposition is convenience through location density rather than cost-effectiveness or advanced financial products. Users seeking the cheapest transfer fees or most innovative features may find better alternatives, though those prioritizing immediate local access will find MoneyGram's network substantial.
address: 903 S César E. Chávez Dr, Milwaukee, WI 53204

company_info.headquarters: 903 S César E. Chávez Dr, Milwaukee, WI 53204

pros: Over 13,000 locations across the United States providing exceptional physical accessibility
Both online and in-person transaction options accommodating different customer preferences
Nationwide money sending and receiving capabilities with established infrastructure
Check cashing services available without requiring a bank account
Money order and wire transfer services available at most locations
Bill payment services integrated into location network
Established brand with decades of operational history and consumer trust
cons: Money transfer fees are typically higher than digital alternatives like Wise or competitors
Check cashing fees are not competitive compared to traditional banks or credit unions
Website provides minimal pricing transparency—fees not displayed upfront
Service quality and wait times vary significantly by location and staffing
Limited product innovation compared to fintech competitors entering the space
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers without access to traditional bank check cashing
Customers prioritizing immediate in-person access over lowest available fees
People sending money domestically who value physical location reliability
Consumers in areas with limited banking infrastructure and few alternatives
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for consumers without bank accounts or those in underserved areas who need immediate access to check cashing, money orders, and transfer services. The primary caveat is that MoneyGram's fees significantly exceed those of bank alternatives and digital competitors, making it a convenience-based choice rather than a cost-effective option for price-sensitive customers.
services: Money transfer (send and receive money nationwide)
Cash pickup services for received transfers
Mobile app for account management and transfers
